title: Daily Home Puja (Nitya Puja)
summary: A simple daily worship routine performed at the home altar. Steps below are the widely shared beginner version; sect and region shape mantras, order, and materials.

steps.title: Prepare yourself and the space
steps.instruction: Bathe or wash hands, face, and feet. Wear clean clothes. Light the altar lamp (diya) and an incense stick.

steps.title: Achamana — ritual sipping of water
steps.instruction: Sip a small spoon of water three times from the right palm while silently naming the deity, to purify speech and intention.

steps.title: Sankalpa — state intention
steps.instruction: Sit facing east or north. Hold a small amount of water, rice, and a flower in the right hand and silently state your name, place, date, and the purpose of the puja before letting the offering fall.

steps.title: Invocation (Avahana)
steps.instruction: Invite the chosen deity into the murti or image with folded hands and a short mantra (commonly an om namah <deity> form).

mantras.meaning: Salutations to Shiva (Shaiva example; replace with your ishta-devata)

steps.title: Offerings (Upachara)
steps.instruction: Offer water for washing feet (padya), hands (arghya), sipping (achamaniya), then bathe (snana) the murti with a few drops of water. Offer clothes or a flower (vastra), sandal paste (gandha), unbroken rice (akshata), flowers (pushpa), incense (dhupa), lamp (dipa), and food (naivedya).

steps.title: Mantra or name recitation
steps.instruction: Chant your chosen mantra or a short stotram 11, 27, or 108 times on a mala.

steps.title: Aarti and closing
steps.instruction: Wave the lit lamp clockwise three or five times. Do pradakshina (circumambulation) if the altar permits. Sprinkle the tirtha water on the head and take the prasad.

variants.label: Shaiva, South Indian home version
variants.instruction: Add vibhuti (sacred ash) on the forehead after Step 4. Chant Panchakshari (Om Namah Shivaya) as the core mantra.

variants.label: Vaishnava home version
variants.instruction: Add tulsi leaves to water and naivedya offerings. Chant the Ashtakshari (Om Namo Narayanaya) or Dvadashakshari mantra.

sources.quote: A leaf, a flower, a fruit, water — whoever offers to me with devotion, that offering of the pure-hearted I accept.
